expectations.text: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 1000 characters
expectations.evidence: Message body (before ---) is approximately 295 characters — well under 1000.

expectations.text: Contains at least one Voss label: 'It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', or 'It feels like'
expectations.evidence: 'It sounds like the real cost isn't the manual work itself...'

expectations.text: Output includes Phase: and Tactic: meta-line
expectations.evidence: Meta-line present: 'Phase: Label' and 'Tactic: Voss label that sharpens Priya's pain...'

expectations.text: Does not drop a quantified NexusPoint result — she hasn't asked for proof yet, still in Label phase
expectations.evidence: No quantified results. No percentages or case study references.

expectations.text: Does not ask for a call or meeting
expectations.evidence: No call ask. Ends with a calibrated follow-up question about their internal process.

expectations.text: Does not pitch NexusPoint services or say 'we can help with this' / 'I can build this for you'
expectations.evidence: No pitch. No mention of NexusPoint or services.
No em dashes. Plain hyphens used in 'isn't the manual work itself - it's that the process...'

expectations.text: Label reframes or sharpens what Priya said — not just repeating her own words back verbatim
expectations.evidence: Priya said 'drowning in manual onboarding... spreadsheets and Looms everywhere.' The label reframes this as 'the process lives in people's heads rather than in a system, so every new hire and every new client restarts from scratch' — sharper, structural framing.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. The label-sharpness assertion is the most important here and correctly enforced.
